title: CloudDash API Authentication
content:
The CloudDash REST API uses Bearer token authentication with organization-scoped API keys. All API requests must include a valid key in the Authorization header.

Creating an API Key: Navigate to Settings > API & Integrations > API Keys > Create Key. Assign a descriptive name (e.g., 'CI/CD Pipeline' or 'Terraform Provider'). Select the key scope: Read Only (GET endpoints only), Read Write (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE on dashboards and alerts), or Admin (includes organization settings). Copy the key immediately — CloudDash displays the full key only once at creation.

Making Authenticated Requests: Include the header Authorization: Bearer cdash_live_XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX in every request. Example using curl: curl -H 'Authorization: Bearer cdash_live_abc123' https://api.clouddash.io/v1/dashboards. The API base URL is https://api.clouddash.io/v1/ for production. Sandbox environment (Enterprise only) uses https://sandbox-api.clouddash.io/v1/.

Key Validation: Invalid or expired keys return HTTP 401 with body {"error": "unauthorized", "message": "Invalid API key"}. Keys revoked by an admin return the same response. Rate-limited requests return HTTP 429 (see KB-014).

Organization Context: API keys are scoped to the organization where they were created. Cross-organization access is not supported. Multi-org users must create separate keys in each organization.

Security Best Practices: Rotate keys every 90 days. Use Read Only scope when write access is unnecessary. Store keys in secrets managers (AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault, GCP Secret Manager) rather than code repositories. Enable IP allowlisting on Enterprise plans to restrict key usage to known CIDR ranges.

OAuth 2.0 for Third-Party Apps: CloudDash supports OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow for marketplace integrations. Register your application at Settings > API & Integrations > OAuth Apps to obtain client_id and client_secret.
